content_en:
in prostration, so increase your du @s in it”.

For this reason, the Prophet (#%) was prohibited by Allah from reciting the Quran while in a state of rvka@ or sujutd, and he (38), in turn, prohibited the Muslims from this also. Ibn ‘Abbas narrated that once the Prophet (3%) lifted the curtain from his house and looked into the mosque, and the people were lined in rows behind Abt Bakr (and this was during the illness from which he (3%) died). He then said:

“O people! There is nothing left of prophethood except a true dream, a Muslim sees it himself or someone else sees it for him (ie. he sees the other person in a dream). And I have been prohibited from reciting the Quran while in ruka@’ or sujitd, so

29Narrated by Muslim and al-Nasa’i, from Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman.

30 Reported by Muslim, Aba Dawid, al-Nasa’i and others, from Aba Hurayrah, as mentioned in Sahih al-Jamt' #1175.

during ruku', glorify your Lord, and during sujad, exert yourself in making du'd, for it is very likely that you will be re-

Therefore,recitation of the Qur’an during these two postures is discouraged, and instead the worshipper should praise and glorify Allah. During sujéd he should increase his du‘ as to his Creator, as they are likely to be responded to.

7. While Reciting al-Fazihah

There are many blessings contained within Sarah a/-Fatihah, and of these is the fact that Allah accepts the du‘a that is present in the Sarah. The person recites it sincerely, fully aware of its meanings. Aba Hurayrah narrated that the Prophet (8) said:

Allah has said: I have divided the salah between My servant and Me in two halves, and My servant will have what he asks for. So when the servant says, ‘AJhamdu lillahi Rabb il-' Alamin’, Allah responds, ‘My servant has praised Me!’ And when he says, ‘Al-Rahman al-Rahim’ Allah responds, ‘My servant has glorified Me’. And when he says, ‘Malik yawm al-Din’, Allah responds, ‘My servant has exalted Me’. And when he says, ‘Iyyaka na‘ budu wa iyyaka nasta'in’, Allah says, “This is between My servant and I, and My servant shall! have what he desires’. And when he says, ‘Ihdina al-Sirat al-Mustaqgim...’ Allah says, ‘This is for My servant, and he will get what he

So in this hadith, there is a great incentive for us to recite seerah al-Fatihah in every prayer with great humility and reflection, so that it is possible that we are given what we ask.

231 Narrated by Muslim, Aba Dawad, and Ahmad as mentioned in Sahih al-

232 Reported by Muslim (# 395) and others.

Likewise, after finishing the recitation of Sarah al-Fatihah in salah, one is encouraged to say, ‘Amin’ out loud, for the Prophet (#8) said:

When the Imam says ‘Amin’ then recite it behind him (as well),

because whoever’s Amin coincides with the Amin of the angels

will have all of his sins forgiven.?

9. dua Before the End of salah

After a person has finished his zashahhud, and before he actually says salam, he should supplicate with any du‘a that he likes, as this . is one of the times of response.

Ibn Mas'id narrates: I was once praying, and the Prophet (3%), Abt Bakr, and ‘Umar (were all present). When I sat down (in the final tashahhud), | praised Allah, then sent salams on the Prophet (#8), then started praying for myself. At this, the Prophet (38), said:

10. Du‘ a After the salah

In the last part of the night, and after the obligatory prayers”.

In fact, the Prophet (3) stressed this time period to those whom he loved, for he said to Mu‘adh ibn Jabal:

33 Reported by al-Bukhari (# 780), Muslim (# 410) and others.

234 Reported by al-Tirmidhi (# 593) who said that it is hasan sahih, and it is as he said. Also see al-Mishkat, # 931.

O Mu'adh! I swear by Allah, I love you. Therefore, do not forget to say after each prayer, ‘O Allah help me to remember

You, and to thank You, and to perfect my worship for You’.”**

It should be mentioned that there is a difference of opinion amongst scholars over the exact meaning of ‘..after the obligatory prayers’, and this stems from the Arabic word that is used to convey the meaning of ‘after’, for the word used is dubur. Some scholars, such as Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, considered the meaning of this hadith to apply to the time period defore one says the final salam of the prayer, and after one has finished reciting the tashahhud. So, according to those that follow this opinion, the word dubur would translate as ‘at the end’, and not, ‘after’,. According to this opinion, the time period would be the same as that mentioned in the previous section.

However, other scholars understand this hadith to mean the time period after the prayer finishes, and the word carries both meanings, as Ibn al-Qayyim mentioned.”

11. dua when the Armies Meet

During this critical period, when the Muslim is facing the enemy in battle, at a place where life and death meet, the dua of a worshipper is accepted by Allah. Proof for this has already been given, and that is the narration of Sahl ibn Sa’d, who confirmed

that the Prophet (3) said:

“Two (du'ds) are never rejected, or rarely rejected: the dua during the call for prayer, and the du’ a during the calamity,

when the two armies attack each other”.

236 Authentic, narrated by Aba Dawid, Ahmad and al-Nasa’i, from Mu‘adh ibn Jabal, and authenticated by al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami' # 7969.

237 See Zad al-Ma' ad, 1/305.

38 Reported by Aba Dawad (# 2540), Ibn Majah, and al-Hakim, from Sah! ibn Sa‘d, and authenticated by al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami’ # 3079.

“Seek the response to your du’ as when the armies mect, and

when the prayer is called, and when rain falls”??

12. An Hour on Friday

There is a special hour on Friday, during which all prayers are accepted and answered by Allah, all Glory and Praise be to Him. This is part of the blessings that Allah has blessed this day with, over all the other days of the week.

Abi Hurayrah narrated that the Prophet (3) said:

“On Friday, there is an hour during which, if any Muslim is standing in front of Allah in salah, and asking Allah for something good, he will be granted his request.”

Then he made a sign with his hands, showing that it was a very short period of time.“

There are numerous opinions as to the exact hour of Friday during which this occurs, but two opinions are the strongest: when the Imam sits down between the two khutbahs until the end of the khutbah, and after the ‘Asr prayer until the Maghrib prayer. Ibn alQayyim preferred the second over the first one.”' This because of the following hadith:

Friday has twelve hours (or parts to it). There is one hour during which ifa Muslim asks Allah anything, Allah will give it to him, so find it during the last hour after “Agro”

This is also the opinion of the vast majority of early scholars as also of the Prophet’s (3%) Companions — that this hour occurs after ‘ Agr prayer, right before sunset.”

239 Reported by al-Shafi'i in his al-Umm (1/223); authenticated in al-Sahihah,

*40 Narrated by al-Bukhari (935) from Aba Hurayrah.

ald Reported by Abu Dawid (# 926 of Sahih al-Sunan) and al-Nasa’i (# 1316 of Sahih al-Sunan).

243 See al-Hilali’s discussion of this point in his book, pp. 50-55.

13. When Waking up at Night

When a person wakes up in the middie of the night, only to go back to sleep again, if he remembers Allah at this time, his du’ a will be accepted.

The Prophet (3%) stated:

Whoever wakes up at night, and says, ‘La ilaha illa Allah Wahdahu la sharika lah, lahu al-mulk wa lahu al-hamd wa huwa ala kulli shay’in Qadir. Alhamdu lillah, wa Subhan Allah, wa la ilaha illa Allah, wa Allahu akbar, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa bi Allah’, and then says, ‘O Allah, forgive me!’ or makes a du'a, then he will be responded to. And if he performs wuda

and prays, then his prayer will be accepted.”**

14. After Performing Wuda The Prophet (3) said:

There is no one amongst you that makes wud#, and does so perfectly, and then says, ‘I testify that there is no deity worthy of worship except Allah. He is alone, having no partners. And I bear witness and testify that Muhammad is His slave and messenger’, except that all eight doors of Paradise are opened for him, and he can enter into it through whichever one he pleases.”

In this hadith, there is an indication that du‘ a at this time has a greater chance of being responded to, as all the doors of Paradise are opened to the beleiver.

15. Before Drinking Zam Zam

Before drinking Zam Zam, one is encouraged to make dw'4, as

the Prophet (#8) said:

The water of Zam Zam is for whatever it has been drunk for.

24 Reported by al-Bukhari (# 1154) and others. 245 Reported by Muslim (# 234) and others.

46 Narrated by Ahmad (3/357) and Ibn Majah (# 3062). AL-Ajlani considered it to be a good isnad and al-Albani considred it sahih (Sahih al-Jami' ,

The meaning of this hadith is that whatever du @ you make before drinking Zam Zam, it will be given you.

16. dua@ During Ramadan

The month of Ramadan is a blessed month, during which the Qur’an was revealed, and the process of inspiration started upon the Prophet (#8). Therefore, the du’ a of Ramadan isa blessed du’ a, and this can be inferred from the Prophet's (3) hadith:

When Ramadan comes, the Doors of Mercy (in one narration: of Paradise) are opened, and the Doors of Hell are closed, and the Shayatin are chained up.”

So it can be inferred from this hadith that du‘ a during Ramadan has a greater chance of being accepted, as the Gates of Paradise and Mercy are opened.
content_id:
dalam sujud, maka perbanyaklah du'a di dalamnya".

Karena alasan inilah, Nabi (#%) dilarang oleh Allah untuk membaca Al-Quran saat dalam keadaan ruku' atau sujud, dan beliau (38) pun melarang umat Muslim dari hal ini juga. Ibn 'Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa suatu ketika Nabi (3%) menyingkap tirai rumahnya dan melihat ke dalam masjid, dan orang-orang sedang berbaris di belakang Abu Bakr (hal ini terjadi saat sakit yang menyebabkan beliau (3%) wafat). Beliau kemudian bersabda:

"Wahai manusia! Tidak ada yang tersisa dari kenabian kecuali mimpi yang benar, seorang Muslim melihatnya sendiri atau orang lain melihatnya untuknya (yakni ia melihat orang lain dalam mimpi). Dan aku telah dilarang membaca Al-Quran saat dalam ruku' atau sujud, maka

29Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dan al-Nasa'i, dari Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman.

30 Dilaporkan oleh Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Nasa'i dan lainnya, dari Abu Hurayrah, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' #1175.

saat ruku', agungkanlah Rabbmu, dan saat sujud, bersungguh-sungguhlah dalam berdu'a, karena sangat mungkin du'amu akan dikabulkan.

Oleh karena itu, membaca Al-Quran saat dua posisi tersebut tidaklah dianjurkan, dan sebaliknya seorang mushalli hendaknya memuji dan mengagungkan Allah. Saat sujud, hendaknya ia memperbanyak du'a kepada Penciptanya, karena du'a saat itu sangat mungkin dikabulkan.

7. Saat Membaca al-Fatihah

Terdapat banyak keberkahan dalam Surah al-Fatihah, dan di antaranya adalah kenyataan bahwa Allah mengabulkan du'a yang terkandung di dalam Surah tersebut. Seseorang membacanya dengan ikhlas, dengan penuh kesadaran akan maknanya. Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (8) bersabda:

Allah berfirman: Aku telah membagi salah antara hamba-Ku dan Aku menjadi dua bagian, dan hamba-Ku akan mendapatkan apa yang ia minta. Maka ketika hamba berkata, 'Alhamdu lillahi Rabb il-'Alamin', Allah menjawab, 'Hamba-Ku telah memuji-Ku!' Dan ketika ia berkata, 'Al-Rahman al-Rahim', Allah menjawab, 'Hamba-Ku telah mengagungkan-Ku'. Dan ketika ia berkata, 'Malik yawm al-Din', Allah menjawab, 'Hamba-Ku telah membesarkan-Ku'. Dan ketika ia berkata, 'Iyyaka na'budu wa iyyaka nasta'in', Allah berfirman, 'Ini antara hamba-Ku dan Aku, dan hamba-Ku akan mendapatkan apa yang ia inginkan'. Dan ketika ia berkata, 'Ihdina al-Sirat al-Mustaqim...' Allah berfirman, 'Ini untuk hamba-Ku, dan ia akan mendapatkan apa yang ia

Maka dalam hadith ini, terdapat dorongan yang besar bagi kita untuk membaca Surah al-Fatihah dalam setiap salah dengan penuh kekhusyukan dan perenungan, agar mungkin kita diberikan apa yang kita minta.

231 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, Abu Dawud, dan Ahmad sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-

232 Dilaporkan oleh Muslim (# 395) dan lainnya.

Demikian pula, setelah selesai membaca Surah al-Fatihah dalam salah, seseorang dianjurkan untuk mengucapkan 'Amin' dengan keras, karena Nabi (#8) bersabda:

Ketika Imam mengucapkan 'Amin' maka ucapkanlah di belakangnya (juga),

karena siapa pun yang ucapan Amin-nya bertepatan dengan ucapan Amin para malaikat

akan diampuni seluruh dosanya.

9. Du'a Sebelum Akhir Salah

Setelah seseorang selesai membaca tashahhud-nya, dan sebelum ia benar-benar mengucapkan salam, hendaknya ia berdoa dengan du'a apa pun yang ia sukai, karena ini adalah salah satu waktu dikabulkannya du'a.

Ibn Mas'ud meriwayatkan: Aku pernah sedang salah, dan Nabi (3%), Abu Bakr, dan 'Umar (semuanya hadir). Ketika aku duduk (pada tashahhud akhir), aku memuji Allah, kemudian mengucapkan salam kepada Nabi (#8), kemudian mulai berdoa untuk diriku sendiri. Maka Nabi (38) berkata:

10. Du'a Setelah Salah

Di penghujung malam, dan setelah salah-salah fardhu.

Bahkan, Nabi (3) menekankan waktu ini kepada orang-orang yang beliau cintai, karena beliau berkata kepada Mu'adh ibn Jabal:

33 Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari (# 780), Muslim (# 410) dan lainnya.

234 Dilaporkan oleh al-Tirmidhi (# 593) yang menyatakan bahwa hadith ini hasan sahih, dan memang demikian adanya. Lihat juga al-Mishkat, # 931.

Wahai Mu'adh! Aku bersumpah demi Allah, aku mencintaimu. Oleh karena itu, jangan lupa untuk mengucapkan setelah setiap salah, 'Ya Allah, bantulah aku untuk mengingatMu, dan untuk bersyukur kepada-Mu, dan untuk menyempurnakan ibadahku kepada-Mu'.

Perlu disebutkan bahwa terdapat perbedaan pendapat di antara para ulama mengenai makna tepat dari '...setelah salah-salah fardhu', dan ini berasal dari kata bahasa Arab yang digunakan untuk menyampaikan makna 'setelah', karena kata yang digunakan adalah dubur. Sebagian ulama, seperti Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, berpendapat bahwa makna hadith ini berlaku pada periode waktu sebelum seseorang mengucapkan salam terakhir dari salah, dan setelah ia selesai membaca tashahhud. Maka, menurut mereka yang mengikuti pendapat ini, kata dubur akan diterjemahkan sebagai 'di akhir', dan bukan, 'setelah'. Menurut pendapat ini, periode waktu yang dimaksud sama dengan yang disebutkan pada bagian sebelumnya.

Namun, ulama lain memahami hadith ini sebagai periode waktu setelah salah selesai, dan kata tersebut mengandung kedua makna, sebagaimana disebutkan oleh Ibn al-Qayyim.

11. Du'a Ketika Dua Pasukan Bertemu

Pada saat kritis ini, ketika seorang Muslim menghadapi musuh dalam pertempuran, di tempat di mana hidup dan mati bertemu, du'a seorang hamba dikabulkan oleh Allah. Bukti untuk hal ini telah diberikan sebelumnya, yaitu riwayat dari Sahl ibn Sa'd, yang mengkonfirmasi

bahwa Nabi (3) bersabda:

"Dua (du'a) tidak pernah ditolak, atau jarang ditolak: du'a saat adzan, dan du'a saat musibah,

ketika dua pasukan saling menyerang satu sama lain".

236 Sahih, diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud, Ahmad dan al-Nasa'i, dari Mu'adh ibn Jabal, dan diotentikasi oleh al-Albani dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 7969.

237 Lihat Zad al-Ma'ad, 1/305.

38 Dilaporkan oleh Abu Dawud (# 2540), Ibn Majah, dan al-Hakim, dari Sahl ibn Sa'd, dan diotentikasi oleh al-Albani dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 3079.

"Carilah pengabulan du'amu ketika dua pasukan bertemu, dan

ketika adzan dikumandangkan, dan ketika hujan turun".

12. Satu Jam pada Hari Jumat

Ada satu jam istimewa pada hari Jumat, di mana semua doa dikabulkan dan dijawab oleh Allah, segala Kemuliaan dan Pujian bagi-Nya. Ini merupakan bagian dari keberkahan yang Allah anugerahkan pada hari ini, melebihi semua hari lainnya dalam seminggu.

Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (3) bersabda:

"Pada hari Jumat, ada satu jam di mana, jika ada seorang Muslim yang sedang berdiri di hadapan Allah dalam salah, dan meminta sesuatu yang baik kepada Allah, permintaannya akan dikabulkan."

Kemudian beliau memberi isyarat dengan tangannya, menunjukkan bahwa itu adalah waktu yang sangat singkat.

Ada banyak pendapat mengenai jam tepat pada hari Jumat di mana hal ini terjadi, namun dua pendapat yang paling kuat adalah: ketika Imam duduk di antara dua khutbah hingga akhir khutbah, dan setelah salah 'Asr hingga salah Maghrib. Ibn al-Qayyim lebih memilih pendapat kedua daripada yang pertama. Hal ini karena hadith berikut:

Hari Jumat memiliki dua belas jam (atau bagian). Ada satu jam di mana jika seorang Muslim meminta sesuatu kepada Allah, Allah akan memberikannya kepadanya, maka temukanlah ia pada jam terakhir setelah 'Asr.

Ini juga merupakan pendapat mayoritas ulama awal dan juga para Sahabat Nabi (3%) — bahwa jam ini terjadi setelah salah 'Asr, tepat sebelum matahari terbenam.

239 Dilaporkan oleh al-Shafi'i dalam al-Umm (1/223); diotentikasi dalam al-Sahihah.

240 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (935) dari Abu Hurayrah.

241 Dilaporkan oleh Abu Dawud (# 926 dari Sahih al-Sunan) dan al-Nasa'i (# 1316 dari Sahih al-Sunan).

243 Lihat pembahasan al-Hilali mengenai hal ini dalam bukunya, hlm. 50-55.

13. Ketika Bangun di Malam Hari

Ketika seseorang bangun di tengah malam, hanya untuk kembali tidur lagi, jika ia mengingat Allah pada saat ini, du'anya akan dikabulkan.

Nabi (3%) menyatakan:

Barangsiapa bangun di malam hari, dan mengucapkan, 'La ilaha illa Allah Wahdahu la sharika lah, lahu al-mulk wa lahu al-hamd wa huwa ala kulli shay'in Qadir. Alhamdu lillah, wa Subhan Allah, wa la ilaha illa Allah, wa Allahu akbar, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa bi Allah', kemudian mengucapkan, 'Ya Allah, ampunilah aku!' atau berdu'a, maka ia akan dikabulkan. Dan jika ia berwudu

dan salah, maka salatnya akan diterima.

14. Setelah Berwudu Nabi (3) bersabda:

Tidak ada seorang pun di antara kalian yang berwudu, dan melakukannya dengan sempurna, kemudian mengucapkan, 'Aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada ilah yang berhak disembah kecuali Allah. Dia Maha Esa, tidak memiliki sekutu. Dan aku bersaksi bahwa Muhammad adalah hamba dan utusan-Nya', kecuali bahwa seluruh delapan pintu Surga dibuka untuknya, dan ia dapat masuk ke dalamnya melalui pintu mana pun yang ia sukai.

Dalam hadith ini, terdapat petunjuk bahwa du'a pada waktu ini memiliki peluang lebih besar untuk dikabulkan, karena seluruh pintu Surga dibuka bagi orang yang beriman.

15. Sebelum Meminum Air Zam Zam

Sebelum meminum air Zam Zam, seseorang dianjurkan untuk berdoa, sebagaimana

Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda:

Air Zam Zam adalah untuk apa pun yang telah diminumnya.

24 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (# 1154) dan lainnya. 245 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (# 234) dan lainnya.

46 Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad (3/357) dan Ibn Majah (# 3062). Al-Ajlani menganggapnya memiliki isnad yang baik dan al-Albani menganggapnya sahih (Sahih al-Jami',

Makna hadith ini adalah bahwa du'a apa pun yang engkau panjatkan sebelum meminum air Zam Zam, niscaya akan dikabulkan bagimu.

16. Du'a Selama Ramadan

Bulan Ramadan adalah bulan yang penuh berkah, di mana Al-Qur'an diturunkan, dan proses wahyu dimulai kepada Nabi (ﷺ). Oleh karena itu, du'a Ramadan adalah du'a yang penuh berkah, dan hal ini dapat disimpulkan dari hadith Nabi (ﷺ):

"Ketika Ramadan tiba, pintu-pintu Rahmat (dalam satu riwayat: Surga) dibuka, dan pintu-pintu Neraka ditutup, serta para Syaitan dibelenggu."

Maka dapat disimpulkan dari hadith ini bahwa du'a selama Ramadan memiliki peluang lebih besar untuk dikabulkan, karena pintu-pintu Surga dan Rahmat telah dibuka.
content_ar: in prostration, لذا increase your du @s in it”. For this العقل, النبي ﷺ (#%) was prohibited by الله from reciting the القرآن الكريم بينما in a state of rvka@ or sujutd, and he (38), in turn, prohibited the المسلمون from this أيضاً. Ibn ‘Abbas narrated that حين النبي ﷺ (3%) lifted the curtain from his house and looked into the المسجد, and the people were lined in rows behind Abt Bakr (and this was خلال the المرض from which he (3%) died). He then said: “O people! There is nothing left of prophethood except a true dream, a مسلم sees it himself or someone else sees it for him (ie. he sees the other person in a dream). And I have been prohibited from reciting the القرآن الكريم بينما in ruka@’ or sujitd, لذا 29Narrated by مسلم and al-Nasa’i, from Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman. 30 Reported by مسلم, Aba Dawid, al-Nasa’i and others, from Aba Hurayrah, as mentioned in صحيح al-Jamt' #1175. خلال الركوع', glorify your Lord, and خلال sujad, exert yourself in making du'd, for it is very likely that you will be re- لذلك,التلاوة of the Qur’an خلال these two postures is discouraged, and instead the worshipper should praise and glorify الله. خلال sujéd he should increase his du‘ as to his Creator, as they are likely to be responded to. 7. بينما Reciting al-Fazihah There are many البركات contained within Sarah a/-Fatihah, and of these is the fact that الله accepts the du‘a that is present in the Sarah. The person recites it sincerely, fully aware of its meanings. Aba Hurayrah narrated that النبي ﷺ (8) said: الله has said: I have divided the الصلاة between My servant and Me in two halves, and My servant will have what he asks for. لذا عندما the servant says, ‘AJhamdu lillahi Rabb il-' Alamin’, الله responds, ‘My servant has praised Me!’ And عندما he says, ‘الرحمن الرحيم’ الله responds, ‘My servant has glorified Me’. And عندما he says, ‘مالك yawm al-Din’, الله responds, ‘My servant has exalted Me’. And عندما he says, ‘Iyyaka na‘ budu wa iyyaka nasta'in’, الله says, “This is between My servant and I, and My servant shall! have what he desires’. And عندما he says, ‘Ihdina al-الصراط al-Mustaqgim...’ الله says, ‘This is for My servant, and he will get what he لذا in this الحديث, there is a great incentive for us to recite seerah al-Fatihah in every الصلاة with great التواضع and reflection, لذا that it is possible that we are given what we ask. 231 Narrated by مسلم, Aba Dawad, and أحمد as mentioned in صحيح al- 232 Reported by مسلم (# 395) and others. Likewise, بعد finishing the التلاوة of Sarah al-Fatihah in الصلاة, one is encouraged to say, ‘آمين’ out loud, for النبي ﷺ (#8) said: عندما the الإمام says ‘آمين’ then recite it behind him (as well), لأن whoever’s آمين coincides with the آمين of the الملائكة will have all of his الذنوب forgiven.? 9. الدعاء قبل the End of الصلاة بعد a person has finished his zashahhud, and قبل he actually says salam, he should supplicate with any du‘a that he likes, as this . is one of the times of response. Ibn Mas'id narrates: I was حين praying, and النبي ﷺ (3%), Abt Bakr, and ‘عمر (were all present). عندما I sat down (in the final التشهد), | praised الله, then sent salams on النبي ﷺ (#8), then started praying for myself. At this, النبي ﷺ (38), said: 10. Du‘ a بعد the الصلاة In the last part of the night, and بعد the obligatory الصلوات”. In fact, النبي ﷺ (3) stressed this time period to those whom he loved, for قال to Mu‘adh ibn Jabal: 33 Reported by البخاري (# 780), مسلم (# 410) and others. 234 Reported by al-الترمذي (# 593) who said that it is حسن صحيح, and it is as قال. أيضاً see al-Mishkat, # 931. O Mu'adh! I swear by الله, I المحبة you. لذلك, do not forget to say بعد each الصلاة, ‘O الله help me to remember You, and to thank You, and to perfect my worship for You’.”** It should be mentioned that there is a difference of opinion amongst العلماء over the exact meaning of ‘..بعد the obligatory الصلوات’, and this stems from the Arabic word that is used to convey the meaning of ‘بعد’, for the word used is dubur. Some العلماء, مثل Shaykh al-الإسلام ابن تيمية, considered the meaning of this الحديث to apply to the time period defore one says the final salam of the الصلاة, and بعد one has finished reciting the التشهد. لذا, وفقاً لـ those that follow this opinion, the word dubur would translate as ‘at the end’, and not, ‘بعد’,. وفقاً لـ this opinion, the time period would be the same as that mentioned in the previous الفصل. غير أن, other العلماء understand this الحديث to mean the time period بعد the الصلاة finishes, and the word carries both meanings, as ابن القيم mentioned.” 11. الدعاء عندما the Armies Meet خلال this البالغ الأهمية period, عندما the مسلم is facing the enemy in battle, at a place where الحياة and الموت meet, the الدعاء of a worshipper is accepted by الله. Proof for this has already been given, and that is the narration of Sahl ibn Sa’d, who confirmed that النبي ﷺ (3) said: “Two (du'ds) are never rejected, or rarely rejected: the الدعاء خلال the call for الصلاة, and the du’ a خلال the calamity, عندما the two armies attack بعضهم بعضاً”. 236 Authentic, narrated by Aba Dawid, أحمد and al-Nasa’i, from Mu‘adh ibn Jabal, and authenticated by al-Albani in صحيح al-Jami' # 7969. 237 See Zad al-Ma' ad, 1/305. 38 Reported by Aba Dawad (# 2540), ابن ماجه, and al-Hakim, from Sah! ibn Sa‘d, and authenticated by al-Albani in صحيح al-Jami’ # 3079. “Seek the response to your du’ as عندما the armies mect, and عندما the الصلاة is called, and عندما rain falls”?? 12. An Hour on Friday There is a special hour on Friday, خلال which all الصلوات are accepted and answered by الله, all Glory and Praise be to Him. This is part of the البركات that الله has blessed this day with, over all the other days of the week. Abi Hurayrah narrated that النبي ﷺ (3) said: “On Friday, there is an hour خلال which, إذا any مسلم is standing in front of الله in الصلاة, and asking الله for something الخير, he will be granted his request.” Then he made a sign with his hands, showing that it was a very short period of time.“ There are numerous opinions as to the exact hour of Friday خلال which this occurs, but two opinions are the strongest: عندما the الإمام sits down between the two khutbahs حتى the end of the khutbah, and بعد the ‘Asr الصلاة حتى the Maghrib الصلاة. Ibn alQayyim preferred the ثانياً over the أولاً one.”' This لأن of the following الحديث: Friday has twelve hours (or parts to it). There is one hour خلال which ifa مسلم asks الله anything, الله will give it to him, لذا find it خلال the last hour بعد “Agro” This is أيضاً the opinion of the vast majority of early العلماء as أيضاً of النبي ﷺ’s (3%) الصحابة — that this hour occurs بعد ‘ Agr الصلاة, الحق قبل sunset.” 239 Reported by al-الشافعي in his al-Umm (1/223); authenticated in al-Sahihah, *40 Narrated by البخاري (935) from Aba Hurayrah. ald Reported by Abu Dawid (# 926 of صحيح al-Sunan) and al-Nasa’i (# 1316 of صحيح al-Sunan). 243 See al-Hilali’s discussion of this point in his الكتاب, pp. 50-55. 13. عندما Waking up at Night عندما a person wakes up in the middie of the night, only to go back to sleep again, إذا he remembers الله at this time, his du’ a will be accepted. النبي ﷺ (3%) stated: Whoever wakes up at night, and says, ‘La ilaha illa الله Wahdahu la sharika lah, lahu al-mulk wa lahu al-hamd wa huwa ala kulli shay’in Qadir. Alhamdu lillah, wa Subhan الله, wa la ilaha illa الله, wa الله أكبر, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa bi الله’, and then says, ‘O الله, forgive me!’ or makes a الدعاء, then he will be responded to. And إذا he performs wuda and prays, then his الصلاة will be accepted.”** 14. بعد Performing Wuda النبي ﷺ (3) said: There is no one amongst you that makes wud#, and does لذا perfectly, and then says, ‘I testify that there is no deity worthy of worship except الله. He is alone, having no partners. And I bear witness and testify that محمد ﷺ is His slave and messenger’, except that all eight doors of الجنة are opened for him, and he can enter into it through whichever one he pleases.” In this الحديث, there is an indication that du‘ a at this time has a greater chance of being responded to, as all the doors of الجنة are opened to the beleiver. 15. قبل Drinking Zam Zam قبل drinking Zam Zam, one is encouraged to make dw'4, as النبي ﷺ (#8) said: The water of Zam Zam is for whatever it has been drunk for. 24 Reported by البخاري (# 1154) and others. 245 Reported by مسلم (# 234) and others. 46 Narrated by أحمد (3/357) and ابن ماجه (# 3062). AL-Ajlani considered it to be a الخير الإسناد and al-Albani considred it صحيح (صحيح al-Jami' , The meaning of this الحديث is that whatever du @ you make قبل drinking Zam Zam, it will be given you. 16. الدعاء@ خلال Ramadan The month of Ramadan is a blessed month, خلال which the Qur’an was revealed, and the process of الوحي started upon النبي ﷺ (#8). لذلك, the du’ a of Ramadan عيسى blessed du’ a, and this can be inferred from النبي ﷺ's (3) الحديث: عندما Ramadan comes, the Doors of الرحمة (in one narration: of الجنة) are opened, and the Doors of النار are closed, and the Shayatin are chained up.” لذا it can be inferred from this الحديث that du‘ a خلال Ramadan has a greater chance of being accepted, as the Gates of الجنة and الرحمة are opened.
